title: URA Private PPI Q1 2026 final reading
content: Singapore private residential PPI rose 0.9% QoQ in Q1 2026, sixth consecutive quarter of growth and revised up from flash estimate of 0.3%. Non-landed properties +1.3%; landed -1.8% (reversed +3.4% prior quarter). OCR non-landed led at +2.2%, RCR +0.8%, CCR +0.6%. Transaction volume crashed to 4,041 deals, -39.7% QoQ. Pipeline: 8,892 units across 20 projects slated for launch in 2Q-4Q 2026.

title: URA Private PPI 2025 full-year result
content: Full-year 2025 private residential PPI rose 3.0%, slowest pace in five years (since 1.9% in 2019). Non-landed full-year 2025: ~2.5%. Landed full-year 2025: +7.6% (four consecutive quarters of growth). Private resale volume hit 14,622 units in 2025 (up 4% from 14,053 in 2024), highest in four years. Sub-sales dropped to 1,055 (proxy for speculation) versus 1,428 in 2024. Developer new sales: 10,815 units, nearly double 2024's 6,469, highest annual sales in four years.

title: HDB Resale 2025 retrospective
content: HDB resale prices rose 2.9% in 2025, slowest growth since 2019 (vs 9.7% in 2024). Q4 2025 prices unchanged for first time since Q1 2020. 26,169 resale flats transacted in 2025 (down 9.7% from 28,986 in 2024). 1,594 million-dollar flats sold in 2025, up 54.7% from 1,035 in 2024. Average million-dollar deal: $1,144,191. Q4 2025 resale volume fell 27.2% QoQ to 5,256 units. Price growth by flat type 2025: 4-room +7.06% psf, 5-room +7.03% psf, 3-room +6.85% psf, 2-room +6.32% psf, executive +5.97% psf.

title: HDB Resale Q1 2026 flash estimate
content: HDB Resale Price Index Q1 2026 dipped 0.1% QoQ, first decline in nearly seven years (since Q2 2019's 0.2% drop). The slowdown reflects increased BTO supply, MOP wave bringing more resale stock, and price resistance among buyers after years of strong gains. Q4 2025 was flat (0.0% QoQ); Q3 2025 was +0.4%.

title: 2026 Private Property Forecast Range
content: Consensus 2026 forecast: overall private PPI +2.5% to +4.5% for the full year. OCR non-landed: +4% to +6% (HDB upgrader demand, MRT-linked launches). RCR: +3% to +5% (owner-occupier core, rental support). CCR: flat to +2% (60% foreigner ABSD continues to suppress). Drivers: easing mortgage rates, ~12,400 completions in 2026 (~65% above 10-year average), cooling measures unchanged. Estimated 23,500-25,500 new launch sales for full year 2026.

title: 2027 Supply Pipeline Outlook
content: 2027 private completion pipeline: ~10,800 units (above 10-year average ~7,500). 2027 new launches reload from sites awarded in 2025: Bedok Rise ($1,330 psf ppr, ~380 units), Telok Blangah Road ($1,326 psf ppr, ~740 units, Feb 2027), Dorset Road ($1,338 psf ppr, ~430 units, Jan 2027), Upper Thomson Parcel A ($1,062 psf ppr, ~595 units, Jan 2027), Lentor Gardens (~530-580 units, Q4 2026 or Q1 2027), Jurong Lake District (~800-1,000 units). 2027 HDB BTO launches: ~15,000+ flats with another ~4,000 shorter-wait units.

title: HDB BTO Supply Plan 2025-2027
content: HDB plans to launch 55,000 BTO flats over 2025-2027 (10% above earlier 50k target). 2026: 19,600 flats across Feb, Jun, Oct exercises, with ~4,000 shorter-wait flats (<3 yrs). 2027: ~15,000+ flats, ~4,000 shorter-wait. Total 2026-2027 new launches: ~35,000 flats (~17,600/year average). National Development Minister Chee Hong Tat noted increased supply will moderate resale prices and possibly enable removal of the 15-month wait-out once prices stabilise.

title: HDB MOP Wave (Resale Supply Unlock) 2025-2028
content: HDB MOP (Minimum Occupation Period) completion wave: 2025: ~6,973 flats; 2026: 13,484 flats; 2027: 18,939 flats; 2028: 21,393 flats. Total 2026-2028 MOP unlock: ~53,816 flats, a 56.1% increase from 34,474 units in 2023-2025 period. This is the primary mechanism expected to moderate resale prices through 2026-2028. Many upgraders who bought BTO 2014-2018 at $400-500k now sitting on $850k+ valuations, with $500-600k proceeds available for private upgrade.

title: 1H 2026 GLS Programme
content: 1H 2026 GLS Programme: 4,575 private residential units on Confirmed List (incl 635 EC units) + 4,610 units on Reserve List = ~9,185 potential units. Confirmed list is 43% above 2021-2023 average of ~3,190 units per GLS programme; 50% above 10-year half-yearly average. Total pipeline (incl ECs) post 1H 2026 release: ~58,600 units. Nine Confirmed sites including: Holland Plain ($1,491 psf ppr), Peck Hay Road (CCR, S$3,200-3,800 expected), Berlayar Drive (RCR, S$2,400-2,900), New Upper Changi Road (S$2,100-2,500), River Valley Green Parcel C (CCR, S$3,500-4,000+), Canberra Drive EC, Sembawang Drive EC.

title: SORA Mortgage Rate Outlook 2026-2027
content: SORA 3M compounded: ~1.0-1.2% Q1 2026 (lowest since Aug 2022). Forecast bottoms ~1.0% by Q2 2026, drifts to ~1.30-1.40% by end-2026 (UOB Peter Chia). 2027: expected to stay below 2% absent Fed shock; Trading Economics models trend toward ~1.5% long-term. Indicative bank packages May 2026: floating SORA + 0.6-0.8% = 1.7-2.0%; 2-3yr fixed 1.45-2.0%. Refinancing window: loans maturing 2026-2027 can pick up ~150-200 bps savings vs 2023 peak. MAS core inflation projected 0.5-1.5% into 2026 supports lower-rate environment.

title: ABSD Rates Unchanged Through 2026
content: ABSD rates from 27 April 2023, unchanged through 2026: SC 0/20/30% (1st/2nd/3rd+); SPR 5/30/35%; Foreigners 60% on any property; Entities/Trustees 65%. Payment within 14 days of OTP (30 days if signed overseas). 2023 ABSD hikes affected ~10% of transactions, primarily reducing CCR luxury investment. Foreigner 60% rate doubled from prior 30%. No further cooling measures announced for 2026. Married SC couple ABSD remission: refundable if first property sold within 6 months of second property's legal completion (resale) or TOP/CSC (new launch).

title: 15-Month Wait-Out for Private-to-HDB Status 2026
content: 15-month wait-out introduced 30 Sep 2022; still in force through 2026. Applies to current/former private property owners buying non-subsidised HDB resale flat. Senior exemption: both spouses 55+ buying 4-room or smaller resale (or 2-room Flexi or Community Care Apartment). BTO instead of resale: 30-month wait. HDB received ~5,500 appeals between Sep 2022 and Mar 2025; ~25% approved, mostly for documentary pre-Sep-2022 OTP commitments or extenuating circumstances. Minister Chee Hong Tat suggested the rule may be reviewed for partial or full removal as resale prices stabilise.

title: HDB Plus/Prime Framework (from Oct 2024)
content: HDB classification framework introduced Oct 2024 BTO exercise: Standard (5yr MOP, no clawback, regular subsidies); Plus (10yr MOP, 6-8% subsidy clawback on resale, in choicer locations); Prime (10yr MOP, ~9% clawback, in choicest locations, SC-only resale, no whole-flat rental). First Prime project: Crawford Heights (Kallang-Whampoa), 312 flats, 3-room from $390k, 4-room from $568k. Subsidy recovery percentage increases with resale price - more upside captured by government. Both Plus and Prime can only be sold after 10-year MOP. Income ceiling for resale buyers also applies for Plus/Prime.

title: CCR/RCR/OCR Performance 2025
content: Singapore non-landed average price psf 2025 vs 2024: All Singapore $1,968→$2,165 (+10.04%). CCR $2,683 with 15.07% growth driven by CCR developer sales rebound and new launches. OCR +2.42%. Resale CCR 2025: 2,592 units sold, highest in four years. Resale composition stayed stable. The 2025 narrative: developer new sales nearly doubled YoY but resale volume only +4%, indicating supply absorption rather than demand surge. Subsales subdued at 6.7% of secondary volume despite strong sentiment, suggesting SSD framework continues to deter speculation.

title: Million-Dollar HDB Flats Trend
content: Million-dollar HDB resale flats: 2024: 1,035 deals. 2025: 1,594 deals (+54.7%). Q3 2025: 480 units, Q4 2025: 351 units (sharp drop). Average million-dollar transaction in 2025: $1,144,191 (vs $1,120,728 in 2024). Concentration in mature estates (Toa Payoh, Bishan, Queenstown, Bukit Merah, Kallang-Whampoa) and central areas. Likely impact: signals continued demand for premium HDB, especially from upgraders willing to pay for location over flat type.

title: 2026 New Launch Pipeline (Quietest in 3 Years)
content: 2026 new launches: ~17 projects, ~8,100 units (down ~30% YoY from 2025's ~23 projects, 11,000+ units). Quietest launch year in at least 3 years. Cause: GLS cycle - sites awarded 2019-2020 already launched in 2023-2025; sites awarded 2022-2023 not yet ready (will launch 2027+). Plus 6-12 month construction delays. CCR/RCR 2026 highlights: UPPERHOUSE Orchard Blvd, W Residences Marina View, 21 Anderson. 2026 launches recording >90% take-up on launch weekend remain common, indicating buyer pipeline is loaded despite low supply.

title: Private Rental Market Q1 2026
content: Q4 2025 URA rental index -1.2% QoQ as expat demand normalised. Q1 2026: rental index rose marginally +0.3%. Expected sideways-to-softer through 2026 due to record completions. Rents normalising from extraordinary 2022-2023 surge (+25-30% YoY peak). Tenant-favourable conditions especially for older non-integrated condos as expat renters rotate to newer stock. Gross yields 2026 indicative: mass-market OCR 3.0-4.0%, city-fringe RCR 2.8-3.5%, prime CCR 2.5-3.2%, older leasehold 3.5-5.0% (with lease decay drag).

title: SSD Holding Period Extension (Cooling Tool)
content: SSD framework reinforced in 2025 with extended holding periods. Effective deterrent against property flipping. Sub-sales remained negligible despite strong sentiment - 1,055 sub-sales in 2025 vs 1,428 in 2024 (6.7% of total secondary market). Confirms calibrated measures anchoring market to long-term fundamentals rather than speculation. Current SSD residential: 12% within 1 year, 8% within 2 years, 4% within 3 years, 0% after.

title: Decoupling Strategy for HDB Upgraders
content: Decoupling: private joint-owned property only (HDB cannot be decoupled except by divorce court order). Cost ~$25-50k (BSD on transferred share + 2 lawyers + admin). Saves $200-700k+ in ABSD typically. Requirements: 2 SEPARATE lawyers (joint counsel is IRAS red flag), market-value transfer with independent valuation, sole-name loan qualification by remaining spouse, 2-4 week gap before second OTP. IRAS may challenge if arrangement is clearly contrived (Section 33 anti-avoidance). Genuine decoupling with economic substance is defensible. Best when keeping current property for rental income while buying second.

title: 6-Month ABSD Remission for SC Married Couples
content: ABSD remission for married couples (at least one SC spouse): buy second property paying 20% ABSD upfront, sell first property within 6 months of second property's legal completion (resale) or TOP/CSC issuance (new launch), apply for refund within 6 months of first property's sale. IRAS does NOT grant general extensions; pandemic-era 6-month extension lapsed. Single SCs, SPR couples, SC+Foreigner couples (except FTA partner citizens: Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland, USA) do NOT qualify. Refund typically issued within 6 weeks of complete application. The remission is the most powerful single ABSD savings tool for SC married upgraders.

title: US Fed Outlook Impact on Singapore 2026-2027
content: US Federal Reserve outlook drives SORA via SGD-USD interest rate parity. Consensus: ~1 Fed cut in 2026 (25bp), slower pace from 2027 onwards. SG GDP growth 2.6% slightly tempers downside. SORA bottoms Q2 2026 ~1.0%, rises to ~1.39% end-2026 (UOB). Risks: if Fed holds rates longer than expected, SORA could rise back above 2% by 2027, pressuring affordability and resale market. If Fed cuts faster (recession scenario), SORA could fall further but at cost of weaker SG GDP and employment - mixed for property.

title: CPF Retirement Sums (BRS/FRS/ERS) 2026-2027
content: CPF Retirement Sums for those turning 55: 2024 BRS $102,900 FRS $205,800; 2025 BRS $106,500 FRS $213,000; 2026 BRS $110,200 FRS $220,400; 2027 BRS $114,100 FRS $228,200. FRS = 2x BRS, fixed for life once you turn 55. Enhanced Retirement Sum (ERS) raised from 3x BRS to 4x BRS from 2025: 2026 ERS $440,800; 2027 ERS $456,400. At 55+, CPF refund on property sale first tops up RA to FRS, with excess flowing to OA. This converts illiquid property equity into lifetime CPF LIFE income.

title: Foreigner Landed Property Rules
content: Foreigners cannot buy mainland landed property without prior LDAU (Land Dealings Approval Unit) approval. Approval discretionary, requires demonstrated economic contribution to Singapore. Process takes weeks to months. Sentosa Cove is the sole exception where foreigners can buy ONE landed unit for own-occupation without LDAU approval. SPR same restriction; LDAU approval required (rarely granted unless meaningful contribution). FTA partner citizens (Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland, USA) get ABSD parity with SC but do NOT get automatic landed ownership exemption. Strata landed in condo developments treated as non-landed for foreigner ownership purposes; foreigners can buy.

title: Construction Cost Pressure 2025-2027
content: Singapore construction costs elevated due to global supply chain, labour constraints, and materials inflation. 6-12 month construction delays common for projects originally slated 2025; many slipped to 2026 or later. Mid-spec condo rebuild cost 2026: $350-500 psf GFA terrace/semi-D, $500-700 detached, $800-1500 luxury/GCB. Basement construction premium $1,200-1,800 psf for basement portion alone. Combined with 12-24 month construction timeline and 10% contingency, landed rebuild ROI calculations need realistic timeline padding.

title: Rental Yield Compression 2026
content: Rental yield compression as wages normalise. Investor maths increasingly anchored on capital appreciation rather than cash flow. Singapore condo gross yields 2026: mass-market OCR 3.0-4.0%, city-fringe RCR 2.8-3.5%, prime CCR 2.5-3.2%, older leasehold or unfashionable areas 3.5-5.0% with lease decay drag. Net yields after maintenance, property tax (non-owner-occupied rates), insurance, agent fees, vacancy buffer, and repairs reserves: subtract 1.0-1.5% from gross. For 99-year leasehold older stock, factor in capital decline from Bala's Curve when computing total return.

title: TDSR/MSR Loan Framework
content: TDSR (Total Debt Servicing Ratio): all debt obligations including new mortgage cannot exceed 55% of gross monthly income. Stress test rate: 4% bank residential, 3% HDB loan, 5% non-residential. Includes other property loans, car loans, credit card min payments (or 3% of balance), personal loans. MSR (Mortgage Servicing Ratio): only for HDB flats and ECs from developer; new mortgage cannot exceed 30% of gross monthly income. LTV: 75% first loan, 45% second, 35% third+. Min cash: 5% first / 25% second+. Max tenure: HDB 25yr (age 65 cap), private 30yr (age 75 cap). Variable income typically haircut 30%.

title: Upgrader Pipeline 2026-2028
content: Upgrader cohort entering market 2026-2028: bought HDB 2014-2018 when 5-room flats were $400-500k. Now selling at $850k-1M typical, walking away with ~$500-600k proceeds after loan repayment and CPF refund. Easily funds down payment on $1.8-2.2M private condo. HDB softness in 2025-2026 is uncomfortable for HDB sellers but not fatal to upgrader pipeline. Even if HDB prices soften $40-50k further, upgrader still walks out with ~$550k - comfortably funding the same private purchase. This is the structural reason MOP wave will NOT pull private prices down.

title: Buy or Wait Decision Framework 2026
content: 2026 base case: not a bull market for property investors but a stable environment for genuine owner-occupiers. Prices grind higher at low single digits. Downside case: if Fed holds rates longer than expected and supply lands faster than absorbed, flattish 2H 2026 possible. Don't try to time the bottom for own-stay purchases. For investment: yield economics weaker than 2010-2018; rely on capital appreciation rather than rental returns. Foreign investment muted by 60% ABSD. Best entry conditions: 75% LTV (first property), SORA at floor (~1% Q2 2026), no immediate cooling measure announcements.

title: Quality of Developer New Launches 2026
content: New launch projects to watch for take-up >70% on launch weekend as quality signal. 2026 launches across OCR (Tampines, Bedok, Upper Thomson, Lentor), RCR (Greater Southern Waterfront, Queenstown, Bayshore), and select CCR (Orchard Boulevard, Marina View). EC pipeline limited: Canberra Drive (~185 units), Sembawang Drive (~450 units), Miltonia Close (~430 units tender closing Apr 2026). EC income ceiling remains $16k; sweet spot for HDB upgraders just above resale eligibility income.

title: Executive Condominium (EC) Outlook 2026
content: EC market 2026: strong MSR-eligible demand. Income ceiling $16k household at purchase. Limited 2026 supply: Canberra Drive EC (~185 units), Sembawang Drive EC (~450 units, North), Miltonia Close EC (~430 units, Yishun, tender closing Apr 2026). After 5-year MOP, ECs can be resold to SC/SPR. After 10 years, fully privatised - resale to foreigners possible (subject to ABSD). Typical EC pricing 2026: $1,350-$1,600 psf. Defensible value play for HDB upgraders earning $13-16k household who don't quite stretch to mass-market private condo.

title: What to Refresh Each Quarter (Workflow)
content: Quarterly refresh checklist: 1. URA full PPI and flash estimate; 2. HDB Resale Index and monthly transactions; 3. Latest GLS Confirmed and Reserve list (1H/2H releases); 4. New launch take-up reports from ERA/OrangeTee/Huttons/PropNex; 5. SORA 3M compounded vs prior quarter and MAS rate outlook; 6. Any IRAS/MND/HDB/MAS policy press release on ABSD, TDSR, LTV, BTO framework; 7. Macro: US Fed dot plot, MAS core CPI, SG GDP guidance; 8. Major project launches and award news. Authoritative sources: URA media releases, HDB resale statistics, IRAS, MAS interest rate data, CPF Board, OneMap, Data.gov.sg.
